The '''''Hammerhead''-class cruiser''' was a type of [[Warship/Legends|warship]] used by the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Old Sith Wars]],<ref name="KOTOR" /> the [[Mandalorian Wars]],<ref name="KOTORC">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series]]</ref> and the [[New Sith Wars]].<ref name="DB:POD" /> One of the most successful warship designs in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history, the ''Hammerhead''-class still saw use in [[Planetary Security Forces|sector defense forces]] millennia after its introduction and inspired many other ship designs, such as the [[Thranta-class corvette|''Thranta''-class corvette]] and [[MC140 Scythe-class main battle cruiser|MC140 ''Scythe''-class main battle cruiser]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

==Characteristics==
[[File:Endar_Spire_bridge.jpg|thumb|left|240px|The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of a ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]]

This ship was a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] and a successor design to the smaller [[Praetorian-class frigate|''Praetorian''-class frigate]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Serving on the line in fleet engagements as a courier and as a [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] of large fleets, it was a workhorse of the Republic and a common sight near Republic-controlled [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. This ship could also undertake independent operations, unlike the [[Foray-class blockade runner|''Foray''-class blockade runner]], which was never observed outside a supporting fleet. ''Hammerhead''s could enter atmospheres and land on planetary surfaces.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The spaceframe resembled the later [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class cruiser]] and the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], though this may have been coincidental. They also strongly resembled the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] era, due to the vertically elongated bridge.<ref name="KOTOR" />

In [[3956 BBY]] the cruisers appeared to possess at least seven [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], with three batteries of two and a single turbolaser cannon mounted atop the hammerhead bridge structure. As demonstrated by the ''[[Endar Spire]]'' and the [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']] some years later, they also appeared to have a number of lighter, point-defense laser cannons for use against small craft mounted at various points along the hull.<ref name="KOTOR" />

They were propelled by four [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]] emitting particles in the orange section of the visible [[Spectrum/Legends|spectrum]] during the wars around 3956 BBY. This changed over time, earlier ships having three engines that emitted blue particles.<ref name="KotOR 8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref>

==History==
===Great Sith War===
Hammerheads were first seen in service in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], where they participated in the [[Battle of Koros Major]].<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> During the restoration period after the war, it was mass-produced at such a scale that [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]] finished a new cruiser every ten days for several years.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

===Mandalorian Wars===
During the [[Mandalorian Wars]] thirty years later, they remained in frontline service, sometimes alongside the [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class command ship]], itself a class made to sell more Hammerheads.<ref name="KOTORHB" /> Hammerheads participated in the Battle of Vanquo, [[Battle of Serroco]] and defended Coruscant, among many other deployments. With a third of the Republic's fleet crushed at [[Malachor V]] during the wars,<ref name="KOTORII">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> and another third, including bigger [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruisers]], taken by [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] to form the basis of [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|his Sith Empire's]] forces,<ref name="KOTOR" /> the ailing Republic government turned to these smaller capital ships to halt the Sith advance.<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>

===Later use===
[[File:HammerheadKnightErrant.jpg|thumb|225px|A ''Hammerhead''-class ship during the [[New Sith Wars]]]]
Throughout the [[Great Galactic War]], [[Galactic War]], and [[New Sith Wars]], the class remained in service. ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers served alongside ''Thranta''-class corvettes, a competitor design, at the [[Second battle of Leritor]].<ref name="The Old Republic: Annihilation">''[[The Old Republic: Annihilation]]''</ref> Another ship, the [[Tanthior (Hammerhead-class)|''Tanthior'']], was shot down over [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the [[Eternal Empire conquest|war with Zakuul]].<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' - Onslaught</ref> During the New Sith Wars, Hammerheads each held a squadron of [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]] and had undergone significant upgrades and design revisions since their debut thousands of years before.<ref name="DB:POD" />
Much like the [[Invincible-class Dreadnaught Heavy Cruiser|''Invincible''-class dreadnaught]], the ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser was one of the longest-serving classes of ship in the Republic military, with versions of it being maintained in active service for at least 3,000 years.<ref name="DB:POD" /><ref name="LCH">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=0960#15875597|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-11-02|time=10:17 AM}}</ref>

One ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser which had been retrofitted into a Jedi temple by Master [[Val Isa/Legends|Val Isa]] before being destroyed in the Mandalorian Wars, the ''Sanctuary'', was rediscovered by a group of Force-sensitive individuals in 0 ABY.<ref name="Lessons from the Past">{{FFG|story=Lessons from the Past|book=Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|text=''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Wreckagemustafar.png|thumb|left|180px|[[Unidentified Hammerhead-class cruiser|Wreckage of a ''Hammerhead''-class ship]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]]]

The ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser was first introduced in the [[2003]] [[Wikipedia:Role-playing video game|role-playing video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''; it is within one such vessel, the ''Endar Spire'', that the game's tutorial level takes place. Near the end of the game, when the ''Ebon Hawk'' crash-lands on [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], the player can see the remains of a ''Hammerhead''-class ship that had crashed into the rocks on the shore. This could have been the vessel used by Revan and Malak during their search for the Star Forge, but this cannot be confirmed. In the game's sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', much more information on this class of vessel is given, including deck plans for crew quarters, a medical bay, and engine rooms.

In the [[Timeline 2: The Mandalorian Blockade is Broken|second timeline trailer]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', a new and more heavily armed variant of the ''Hammerhead''-class is seen above [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. This was later revealed to be a separate class, the [[Thranta-class corvette|''Thranta''-class]].

[[Leland Chee]] has confirmed that the ships seen in the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' games are of the same class that was named in ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', but that the version of the book and that of the game may have different specifications, considering that they are used approximately three thousand years apart from one another.<ref name="LCH" />

The wreckage of a ''Hammerhead''-class vessel can be seen on the volcanic world of [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] in the expansion for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', [[Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan|''Trials of Obi-Wan'']]. How it came to be there is never explained, however, with the implication that it is a long-crashed Republic warship.

The ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser was the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] inspiration for the [[canon]] [[Sphyrna-class Hammerhead corvette|''Sphyrna''-class Hammerhead corvette]],<ref name="Princess Trivia">{{SW|url=series/star-wars-rebels/a-princess-on-lothal-trivia-gallery|text="A Princess on Lothal" Trivia Gallery}}</ref> which made its first appearance in "[[A Princess on Lothal]]," the twelfth episode of the [[Star Wars Rebels Season Two|second season]] of the animated television series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'',<ref name="Episode Guide">{{SW|url=series/star-wars-rebels/a-princess-on-lothal-episode-guide|text="A Princess on Lothal" Episode Guide | Star Wars Rebels}}</ref> released on [[January 20]], [[2016]].<ref name="Leia Scoop">{{WebCite|url=https://www.tvinsider.com/64379/star-wars-rebels-scoop-princess-leia-set-to-appear-on-the-disney-xd-animated-series|text=''Star Wars Rebels'' Scoop: Princess Leia Set to Appear on the Disney XD Animated Series|author=Sands, Rich|date=2016-01-11|work=TV Insider|archivedate=20200307164210}}</ref>
